_schema.purpose: Operator-committed snapshot of Teams deposit state per sector. Overwritten by the operator's snapshot script (not by collect.R).

fields.snapshot_taken_at: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the operator's last refresh
fields.snapshot_source: How the operator generated this snapshot (e.g. 'manual', 'teams_inventory.ps1')

<code>.deposited: boolean — whether the canonical file exists at the expected shared Teams path
<code>.rows: integer — observation count in the deposited file (null if not deposited)
<code>.indicators: integer — distinct indicator count (null if not deposited)
<code>.size_mb: numeric — file size on Teams (null if not deposited)
<code>.path_teams: string — canonical shared Teams path (internal path withheld)
<code>.deposit_date: YYYY-MM-DD — date producer last deposited (null if not deposited)

nt.notes: 1.16M rows x 40 indicators on Teams (operator-confirmed today)

hva.notes: 214k rows x 23 indicators (reviewer-mode overwrite incident filed as cso-toolkit#54)

im.notes: not deposited to Teams (reviewer ran locally to repo)

ws.notes: not deposited to Teams (reviewer ran locally to repo; 3 outputs hhs/sch/hcf)

mnch.notes: 15k rows x 27 indicators on Teams; CRITICAL: a derived output is written directly to canonical raw storage, bypassing dw_save

cme.notes: no Teams deposit expected (sector publishes via a separate regional-aggregate path)

ed.notes: 107k rows x 16 indicators on Teams (step 2/5 only; 3/5 blocked on the World Bank API on the reviewer network)

wt.notes: 6 indicators in scope; pipeline BLOCKED on a Java dependency + an Excel column-schema mismatch + an undeposited upstream population cache

ecd.notes: 8 indicators in scope; pipeline BLOCKED on producer-side machine paths + an undeposited input workbook on Teams

fixes_applied: activated 19 commented-out stages (import, aggregation, target, and format-merge steps) in a sector script
added the openxlsx, countrycode, and glue libraries to the lean test driver (these are loaded by the profile in production but absent in lean-driver test mode)
toolkit_findings: dw_url_allowlist correctly blocked an unallowlisted URL with a non-fatal warning

fixes_applied: dw_root() shim (cso-toolkit#53; toolkit gap to be fixed in v0.4.6)
dwMetaData path corrected to the 01_dw_prep metadata location (per profile convention, not the 012_codes metadata location)
toolkit_findings: CRITICAL: dw_save reviewer-mode mode-lock did NOT engage on a cloud-synced shared path. A producer output was overwritten by this reviewer-mode run. Filed as cso-toolkit#54 (HIGH severity, v0.4.6)

fixes_applied: dw_frozen_root set explicitly under dwRawData (profile-only convention)
toolkit_findings: Reviewer-mode mode-lock honored (output went to _local/im/output/ correctly because sector writes to a repo-relative path explicitly)
Reviewer-mode mode-lock honored (sector writes to a repo-local working path via dwWrkData)
ws.scope_note: A regional-aggregation sector script is Phase-2 work per the orchestrator comments and is NOT wired into the current conductor; not part of this replication scope

toolkit_findings: CRITICAL: sector bypasses cso-toolkit entirely. A sector script writes a derived CSV directly into canonical raw storage in a shared Teams folder — this mixes derived output with canonical raw inputs. Reviewer-mode mode-lock cannot engage when sector code does not call dw_save.
mnch.blockers_for_dbm: Route the derived CSV write through dw_save (it currently writes directly to canonical raw storage, bypassing mode-lock).

fixes_applied: Installed shinyregionalaggregate from unicef-drp/Shiny_regional_aggregate via remotes::install_github
toolkit_findings: CME bypasses cso-toolkit; uses data.table::fread + shinyregionalaggregate package directly. No mode-lock applies because no dw_save call. Output written to repo-local working storage correctly via direct file path.
cme.blockers_for_dbm: If the reviewer-mode contract should engage, the sector must adopt dw_save / dw_use (currently bypasses cso-toolkit). Not a regression — forward-investment for v0.4.x.

ed.halt_reason: World Bank API requests were throttled on the reviewer network after most indicators were successfully fetched; the remaining request failed even after retries.
fixes_applied: PR #151 merged (resolves merge-conflict parse blockers from prior smoke test)
Fixed an undefined-variable typo in a sector script
Re-enabled a disabled World Bank fetch block in a sector script (it was commented out, leaving a downstream variable undefined)
Staged producer-side input files from a shared Teams folder to repo-local dwRawData/ed/
Replaced the upstream fetch helper with a direct request (the helper's default timeout was too short)
toolkit_findings: STEP 2 wrote to a canonical shared path in a shared Teams folder — same mode-lock issue as HVA (cso-toolkit#54)
ed.blockers_for_dbm: Three DBM items: (1) World Bank API throttled on the reviewer network — allowlist or cache; (2) an undefined-variable typo in a UIS aggregation script; (3) a disabled World Bank fetch block leaves a downstream variable undefined.

wt.halt_reason_1: A sector script depends on library(xlsx), which needs a Java runtime that is not available in the reviewer environment.
wt.halt_reason_2: A sector script expects a different Excel column schema than the canonical shared Excel file provides — likely the wrong sheet/file, or a producer working copy that differs from the deposited one.
wt.halt_reason_3: A WT classification script requires a cached upstream population artifact that exists only on the producer side, not in shared storage.
fixes_attempted: Installed the required R packages (the Java-dependent Excel package still fails on Java load)
Ported the sector scripts from the Java-dependent Excel package to openxlsx (pure-R, no Java)
Added a read-shim so the openxlsx reader matches the prior call signature
Removed an environment-clearing call that wiped driver-set globals
Set the required path globals (dwCodes and the shared-storage path globals) explicitly
wt.blockers_for_dbm: Three DBM-actionable items: (1) Java dependency — a sector script needs library(xlsx); the sector lead should port it to openxlsx (pure-R, no Java) so the pipeline runs without a Java runtime; (2) an Excel column-schema mismatch — sector code expects a different column schema than the canonical shared Excel file provides, so the sector lead must reconcile; (3) an undeposited upstream population cache — a WT script requires a cached population artifact that exists only on the producer side, so it must be deposited to shared storage or its regeneration documented.

ecd.halt_reason: An ECD prep script hardcodes input/output directories to a producer-side personal path, so none of them resolve on the reviewer's machine. Additionally, a sector script depends on library(xlsx), which requires Java (unavailable on the reviewer machine), same as wt.
fixes_attempted: Identified the hardcoded producer-side paths in a sector script (input/output/working/region-group directories)
Searched a shared Teams folder for the source workbook referenced by the indicator scripts — the workbook does not exist in shared storage (only unrelated CSV variants are present)
Searched for ecd-specific raw inputs — none located at the path the script expects
ecd.blockers_for_dbm:
Four DBM-actionable items:
  1. CRITICAL: Replace the hardcoded producer-side paths in a sector script with profile-driven globals (dwRawData, dwCodes, dwWrkData, dwMetaData). The current code is portable only on one producer's machine.
  2. Deposit the producer-side source workbook (referenced by all six ECD indicator scripts) to a shared canonical location so reviewers can read it. The workbook is currently only on the producer side.
  3. Java install OR xlsx -> openxlsx port (same as wt).
  4. The script bypasses cso-toolkit entirely (no dw_use, dw_save). For v0.4.x compliance the sector lead must adopt dw_io contract.

actions.title: CME: adopt the cso-toolkit dw_io contract (forward-investment for v0.4.x)
actions.blocker_for_dbm: CME currently bypasses cso-toolkit (direct file IO); no reviewer-mode mode-lock applies.
actions.description: Refactor the CME sector drivers to use dw_save / dw_use so reviewer-mode mode-lock and dw_use provenance engage. Not a regression — forward-investment for v0.4.x adoption. Detail in the private DW-Production tracker.

actions.title: cso-toolkit#51: cso_require envelope
actions.description:
Add cso_require() envelope to standardise required-package loading across
sector drivers. Tracked at cso-toolkit#51.

actions.title: cso-toolkit#52: CRLF normalisation
actions.description:
Cross-platform CRLF normalisation hardening across cso-toolkit IO helpers.
Tracked at cso-toolkit#52.

actions.title: cso-toolkit#53: dw_root() wrapper / shim
actions.blocker_for_dbm: Reviewer had to write a dw_root() shim to replicate HVA; toolkit gap to be fixed in v0.4.6
actions.description:
HVA replication required a hand-written dw_root() shim. cso-toolkit should
expose this as a first-class API in v0.4.6.

actions.title: cso-toolkit#54: dw_save reviewer-mode mode-lock can miss cloud-synced shared paths
actions.blocker_for_dbm: Reviewer-mode replay can overwrite a producer output when the shared path is cloud-synced; affects HVA and ED.
actions.description: Reviewer-mode replay overwrote a producer output because dw_save mode-lock did not engage on a cloud-synced shared path (the path classifier was bypassed). Any sector writing to such a path is at risk. Tracked at cso-toolkit#54 (HIGH, v0.4.6 milestone).

actions.title: ECD: adopt the cso-toolkit dw_io contract (currently bypasses)
actions.blocker_for_dbm: ECD code uses neither dw_use nor dw_save; v0.4.x compliance needs dw_io adoption.
actions.description:
ECD bypasses cso-toolkit (no dw_use / dw_save). For v0.4.x compliance the
sector lead must adopt the dw_io contract. Detail in the private
DW-Production tracker.

actions.title: ECD: deposit the producer-side source workbook to a shared location
actions.blocker_for_dbm: The ECD indicator scripts read a source workbook that exists only on the producer side, not in shared storage.
actions.description:
The ECD indicator scripts depend on a source workbook held only on the
producer side. Deposit it to a shared canonical location so reviewers can
read it. Detail in the private DW-Production tracker.

actions.title: ECD: replace producer-side machine paths with portable profile globals
actions.blocker_for_dbm: The ECD prep script hardcodes producer-side machine paths; portable only on one machine.
actions.description:
The ECD prep script hardcodes input/output directories to a producer-side
personal path, so it runs only on one machine. Replace them with the
profile-driven path globals (dwRawData, dwCodes, dwWrkData, dwMetaData).
Detail in the private DW-Production tracker.

actions.title: ECD: install Java OR port xlsx -> openxlsx
actions.owner: UNICEF IT (Java) OR ECD sector lead (port)
actions.blocker_for_dbm: library(xlsx) requires Java; same problem as wt
actions.description:
Same blocker as wt-java-or-openxlsx. Either IT installs Java/JRE on the
reviewer machine, or sector lead ports from xlsx to openxlsx.

actions.title: ED: World Bank API access — allowlist or cache
actions.blocker_for_dbm: The ED pipeline halts mid-run because upstream World Bank API requests are throttled on the reviewer network.
actions.description: ED steps for Learning Poverty / FLS require the World Bank API, which is throttled on the reviewer network. Options: (a) allowlist the API; (b) cache the data once off-network and commit a frozen snapshot; (c) run those steps off the corporate network. Detail in the private DW-Production tracker.

actions.title: ED: re-enable the World Bank fetch block
actions.blocker_for_dbm: A disabled fetch block leaves a downstream variable undefined; the script crashes without it.
actions.description: The World Bank fetch block in an ED prep script is commented out, but downstream code uses its result. Re-enable it or guarantee the data is populated upstream first. Detail in the private DW-Production tracker.

actions.title: ED: fix an undefined-variable typo in a UIS aggregation script
actions.blocker_for_dbm: Sector code references an undefined variable (a typo) and crashes.
actions.description: An ED UIS/SDG aggregation script references a misspelled variable name and crashes. Fix the typo in the sector code. Detail in the private DW-Production tracker.

actions.title: MNCH: route the raw CSV write through dw_save
actions.blocker_for_dbm: MNCH writes a derived output directly (no dw_save), so reviewer-mode mode-lock cannot engage.
actions.description: An MNCH aggregation step writes a derived CSV directly into canonical raw storage, bypassing dw_save (no mode-lock) and mixing raw with derived. Route it through dw_save to a working path with sector provenance. Detail in the private DW-Production tracker.

actions.title: WT: remove the Java dependency (port xlsx → openxlsx)
actions.owner: UNICEF IT (Java) OR WT sector lead (port)
actions.blocker_for_dbm: A sector script needs Java (library(xlsx)), which is not available on the reviewer machine.
actions.description: A WT sector script depends on library(xlsx), which needs Java (unavailable on the reviewer machine). Either IT installs a JRE, or the sector lead ports to openxlsx (pure-R, no Java) — recommended. Detail in the private DW-Production tracker.

actions.title: WT: deposit the upstream population cache or document its regeneration
actions.blocker_for_dbm: A WT script requires a cached upstream artifact that is not in shared storage.
actions.description:
A WT classification script requires a cached population artifact that exists
only on the producer side. Deposit it to shared storage or document how a
reviewer regenerates it. Detail in the private DW-Production tracker.

actions.title: WT: reconcile the Excel input schema (sector code vs shared file)
actions.blocker_for_dbm: Sector code expects different columns than the shared Excel file provides.
actions.description: WT sector code expects a different column schema than the canonical shared Excel file provides — likely the wrong sheet/file, or a producer working copy that differs from the deposited one. Sector lead to reconcile. Detail in the private DW-Production tracker.
